name: ask-find-question
title: Question Clarify
description: Turn fuzzy intent into one clear problem statement and a small question handle. Use when the user does not know how to ask yet, feels mentally foggy, or needs the question boundary tightened before deeper design.
purpose: Convert a vague thought into a bounded problem statement with a few stable question anchors.
when_to_use: Need to turn a fuzzy thought into one clear question handle.
Need to reduce mental overload before deeper question design.
Need a bounded problem statement rather than broad ideation.
do_not_use: Already have a clear bounded question.
Need repository analysis, implementation, or review.

required_inputs.name: RAW_TOPIC
required_inputs.description: Messy topic, rough thought, or problem statement draft.

required_inputs.name: AUDIENCE
required_inputs.description: Who the future answer or decision is for.

required_inputs.name: CONSTRAINTS
required_inputs.description: Known time, resource, domain, or evidence constraints.

input_contract_notes: RAW_TOPIC can be messy; the skill should reduce ambiguity instead of demanding a polished first draft.
If the user does not provide explicit constraints, infer only the single most limiting constraint from the prompt and mark it as inferred.
Ask what should be avoided before expanding ideal outcomes.

structured_outputs.name: FOG_KEYS
structured_outputs.description: Three concrete anchor words that represent the core issue.

structured_outputs.name: NEGATIVE_SPACE
structured_outputs.description: What the future question should avoid or not become.

structured_outputs.name: PRIMARY_CONSTRAINT
structured_outputs.description: Single dominant constraint that most limits the question.

output_contract_notes: FOG_KEYS should stay short and concrete, not abstract themes.
PROBLEM_STATEMENT should express one bounded question direction, not a list of possible asks.
NEXT_RECOMMENDED_SKILL should usually point to ask-break-it-down unless the question is already bounded enough to ask.
neutrality_rules: Do not answer the topic while clarifying the question.
Separate what the user wants from what the user wants to avoid.
If the prompt stays ambiguous, narrow to one problem statement instead of inventing extra goals.
execution_constraints: Reduce the thought to three anchors, one dominant constraint, and one problem statement.
Prefer the shortest clear wording that helps the user take control of the question.
Do not expand into solution advice or answer content.
mandatory_rules: Keep FOG_KEYS to three items unless the prompt gives fewer usable anchors.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>handoff>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,quality-gates{docs},deterministic-output | lens: inversion-focus | output: md(contract=v1)]
lens_rationale: Question clarification should lower cognitive load, define the negative boundary first, and collapse ambiguity into one usable problem statement.
